# SDH ComplianceApp

![Version](https://img.shields.io/badge/version-2.6.0-blue)
![.NET](https://img.shields.io/badge/.NET-10.0-purple)
![Platform](https://img.shields.io/badge/platform-Windows%20x64-lightgrey)
![Transport](https://img.shields.io/badge/IPC-gRPC%20%2F%20HTTP%2F2-orange)

Windows security compliance assessment suite. Evaluates OS hardening, endpoint agents, encryption,
patch status, local accounts, firewall rules, and policy enforcement — then produces a compliance
report and optionally pushes results to the Security Data Hub (SDH) API.

| Stat | Value |
|------|-------|
| Main projects | 5 |
| Compliance domains | 16 |
| Data transformers | ~207 |
| gRPC RPCs | ~120 |

---

## Architecture

Two-process model. The WPF client and the Windows Service communicate exclusively via gRPC over HTTP/2 on localhost (default port **5250**).

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    WTS["Windows Task Scheduler\n(every 6 h + logon)"]
    USER["User (interactive)"]

    subgraph Client ["ComplianceApp.exe  ·  WPF  ·  net10.0-windows"]
        direction TB
        MVM["MainViewModel\n(orchestration)"]
        DC["DataCollector\n(~207 parallel transformers)"]
        VT["IValueTester\n(regex / version / Lua)"]
        MVM --> DC --> VT
    end

    subgraph Service ["ComplianceService.exe  ·  Windows Service  ·  net10.0-windows"]
        direction TB
        GRPC["ComplianceGrpcService\n(17 partial classes)"]
        DS["DataService"]
        MC["IMemoryCache\n(1 min TTL)"]
        GRPC --> DS --> MC
    end

    subgraph Collectors ["System data sources"]
        WMI["WMI / CIM"]
        REG["Registry"]
        EVT["Event Log"]
        API["Win32 API / BitLocker"]
        NET["Network stack"]
    end

    WE["WorkflowEngine.App.exe\n(OS Hardening)"]
    SDH["SDH REST API"]
    FILE["Local file export"]

    WTS -->|"-NoMainWindow"| Client
    USER -->| interactive | Client
    Client <-->|"gRPC / HTTP2\nlocalhost:5250"| Service
    DS --> Collectors
    DS -->|"CliWrap\n--web true --auto-close true"| WE
    WE -->|"HTTP results"| DS
    VT -->|"export"| SDH
    VT -->|"export"| FILE
```

### Projects

| Project | Role | Target |
|---------|------|--------|
| **ComplianceApp** | WPF client — UI, orchestration, evaluation engine | `net10.0-windows` |
| **ComplianceService** | Windows Service — gRPC server, system data collection | `net10.0-windows` |
| **ComplianceApp.Shared** | Contracts (`IDataService`), DTOs, enums · NuGet `SDH.ComplianceApp.Shared` | `net10.0` |
| **ComplianceApp.Protos** | Auto-generated gRPC stubs from `protos/compliance/v1/` | `net10.0` |
| **WorkflowEngine** | On-demand hardening workflow executable | `net10.0-windows` |

---

## Data Collection Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    actor Scheduler as Task Scheduler
    participant App as ComplianceApp.exe
    participant Adapter as GrpcDataServiceAdapter
    participant Svc as ComplianceService
    participant Cache as IMemoryCache
    participant WMI as WMI / Registry / API
    participant WE as WorkflowEngine.App
    participant SDH as SDH API / File

    Scheduler->>App: launch -NoMainWindow
    App->>App: load / update Win10.json definitions
    App->>Adapter: open GrpcChannel (localhost:5250)

    loop ~207 transformers in parallel (Task.WhenAll)
        App->>Adapter: IDataService.GetXxx()
        Adapter->>Svc: gRPC RPC
        Svc->>Cache: hit?
        alt cache miss
            Cache-->>Svc: miss
            Svc->>WMI: query
            WMI-->>Svc: raw data
            Svc->>Cache: store (TTL 1 min)
        end
        Cache-->>Svc: cached value
        Svc-->>Adapter: Protobuf response
        Adapter-->>App: DTO
    end

    opt OSHardening items
        App->>Adapter: GetWorkflowResults()
        Adapter->>Svc: gRPC RPC
        Svc->>WE: spawn (--web true --auto-close true)
        WE-->>Svc: HTTP results
        Svc-->>Adapter: WorkflowRuleResult[]
        Adapter-->>App: DTO
    end

    App->>App: IValueTester evaluates each item
    App->>SDH: export results
    App->>App: Shutdown(0)
```

---

## Compliance States

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    direction LR
    [*] --> Hidden : item defined
    Hidden --> NotEvaluated : definitions loaded
    NotEvaluated --> NotApplicable : RemoveWhen = true
    NotEvaluated --> Loading : evaluation starts
    Loading --> Compliant : value matches rule
    Loading --> Warning : partial match
    Loading --> NotCompliant : value fails rule
    NotApplicable --> [*]
    Compliant --> [*]
    Warning --> [*]
    NotCompliant --> [*]
```

| Code | State | Hex |
|------|-------|-----|
| 0 | `Hidden` | `0x00` |
| 1 | `NotEvaluated` | `0x10` |
| 2 | `NotApplicable` | `0x20` |
| 3 | `Compliant` | `0x30` |
| 4 | `Warning` | `0x40` |
| 5 | `NotCompliant` | `0x50` |
| — | `Loading` (transient) | `0xFF` |

---

## Compliance Domains

16 `IDataService` sub-interfaces, each mapped to one or more gRPC proto contracts.

```mermaid
mindmap
  root((ComplianceApp))
    Endpoint Agents
      SentinelOne
      SEP / Symantec
      SCCM
      Cloud / MDM
      CyberArk EPM
      LAPS
    OS & Firmware
      Operating System
        Services inventory
        Applications inventory
        Browser Extensions inventory
        IDE Extensions inventory
      GPO
      BIOS / Firmware
      Updates
    Identity & Access
      Active Directory
      Local Accounts
    Data Protection
      BitLocker / FVE
      Firewall
    Hardware and Network
      Hardware
      Networking
    WorkflowEngine
      OS Hardening
```

| Group | Domain | Key checks |
|-------|--------|-----------|
| **Endpoint Agents** | SentinelOne | Agent version, paths, SDK messages, Security Center registration |
| | SEP (Symantec) | Definition versions, agent version, policy group, Security Center registration |
| | SCCM | Client health, site/MP info, inventory cycle, component status |
| | Cloud / MDM | Azure AD join state, Intune enrollment, co-management, device IDs, last sync |
| | CyberArk EPM | Driver status, agent version, dispatcher URL |
| | LAPS | Windows LAPS & legacy LAPS status, password rotation |
| **OS & Firmware** | Operating System | Build, patch level, TLS/cipher suites, pending reboots, SSPI security packages, services inventory, applications inventory, browser extensions inventory, IDE extensions inventory |
| | GPO | Applied group policies, GP extensions list |
| | BIOS / Firmware | UEFI boot, Secure Boot, Credential Guard, firmware version |
| | Updates | Managed/unmanaged update status, Windows Update service state, pending reboot |
| **Identity & Access** | Active Directory | Domain name, site membership |
| | Local Accounts | Local users, admin & RDP group membership, user profiles, active sessions |
| **Data Protection** | BitLocker / FVE | Encryption status & percentage, key protectors, DRA, escrow/rotation, audit events |
| | Firewall | Windows Firewall profiles, WFP rules, Security Center AV/FW product registration |
| **Hardware and Network** | Hardware | CPU, RAM, disks, chassis, virtualization flag, screen resolution, serial number |
| | Networking | IP addresses, MAC addresses |
| **WorkflowEngine** | OS Hardening | Hardening rules evaluated by WorkflowEngine (`OSHardening` workflow) |

---

## Evaluation Engine

Three comparers implement `IValueTester`, selected per item by `TestCondition`:

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    TC["Item\nTestCondition + TestValue"]
    TC -->|"regex pattern"| P["PatternComparer\nRegex match"]
    TC -->|"date / version"| PS["ParsedStringComparer\nTyped comparison"]
    TC -->|"Lua script"| LUA["MoonSharpLuaComparer\nFull Lua 5.2 execution"]
    P --> S["ComplianceState"]
    PS --> S
    LUA --> S

    TC2["RemoveWhen\n(Lua predicate)"] -->|"true → suppress item"| HIDE["Hidden"]
```

| Comparer | When to use |
|----------|-------------|
| `PatternComparer` | String values, version patterns, enumerated states (regex) |
| `ParsedStringComparer` | Date / version typed comparisons ("older than 30 days", "≥ 1.2.3") |
| `MoonSharpLuaComparer` | Complex multi-value logic, conditional rules, `RemoveWhen` predicates |

---

## Periodic Execution & Scheduling

ComplianceApp does not run a continuous loop. Each audit is a **single pass** triggered by Windows
Task Scheduler — the process exits when done.

### Observed runtime — telemetry sample

> Run captured on **EMEAATTN8SO0M9S** · 2026-04-22 · definitions profile: `Win10-Laptop.json`

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Start | 2026-04-22 11:46:54 UTC |
| Export | 2026-04-22 11:47:15 UTC |
| **Total wall-clock time** | **21.1 seconds** |
| Transformers executed | 129 |
| Transformer success rate | 100 % |

#### Transformer duration breakdown

```mermaid
gantt
    title Actual audit timeline — EMEAATTN8SO0M9S (2026-04-22, total 21s)
    dateFormat  ss
    axisFormat  %Ss

    section Startup
    Process start + definitions load   : 00, 2s

    section Parallel collection (Task.WhenAll)
    Most transformers — gRPC + WMI     : 02, 12s
    OSHardening — WorkflowEngine HTTP  : 02, 12s
    SCCM Client Health — WMI heavy     : 02, 12s
    Windows Update pending list        : 02, 10s
    All transformers complete          : milestone, 14, 0s

    section Output
    Evaluation + export + Shutdown     : 14, 7s
    Process exits                      : milestone, 21, 0s
```

| Duration bucket | Typical items | Count | Time range |
|----------------|---------------|-------|-----------|
| **< 15 ms** | Cached / trivial lookups | ~10 | 3 ms – 13 ms |
| **1 – 2 s** | Standard gRPC + WMI reads | ~115 | 1.1 s – 1.9 s |
| **2 – 3 s** | Compound or multi-query items | ~1 | ~2.7 s |
| **9 – 12 s** | Heavy WMI (SCCM, Windows Update) | 4 | 9.1 s – 11.7 s |

> The wall-clock time is dominated by the **SCCM WMI queries** (~11.7 s each), which run in
> parallel with all other transformers. Removing or disabling SCCM checks would bring the
> audit below ~3 s.

### Scheduled tasks (`Scheduler.json`)

| Task | Triggers | Executable | Notes |
|------|----------|------------|-------|
| **Automatic Execution** | Time (every **6 h**, random delay **15 min**) + Logon | `ComplianceApp.exe -NoMainWindow` | Main periodic audit. `StopAtDurationEnd` prevents overlap. |
| **Notification** | None (manual / on-demand) | `ComplianceApp.exe` | Interactive / user-triggered runs. |

Tasks are registered under `\ComplianceApp` in Windows Task Scheduler.
`ScheduledTaskSyncHostedService` syncs them from `Scheduler.json` **once at service startup**.
Only executables in `SchedulerAllowedCommands` (appsettings) are permitted.

---

## Performance Impact on the Workstation

> `ComplianceService.exe` is the only **persistent** process. It is a dormant gRPC server at
> idle — it only consumes resources when `ComplianceApp.exe` is actively calling it during an
> audit. Between audits, no compliance-related process is running.

| Mechanism | Description | Benefit |
|-----------|-------------|---------|
| **Headless mode (`-NoMainWindow`)** | No UI thread, no WPF window rendered during scheduled runs. | Zero GPU/rendering overhead |
| **Single-pass execution** | `ComplianceApp.exe` starts, runs once, then exits. | No memory footprint between audits |
| **Random 15-min delay** | Staggers fleet runs after reboots or policy pushes. | No synchronised load spikes |
| **Parallel `Task.WhenAll`** | All ~207 transformers run concurrently via the thread pool. | Short wall-clock time per audit |
| **`IsEvaluating` guard** | Blocks a second concurrent run via `CanExecute`. | No double-process contention |
| **Service-side `IMemoryCache` (1 min)** | WMI/CIM/Registry reads cached and shared across all transformers. | No redundant WMI queries |
| **Client-side gRPC cache (10 s)** | `[Cache]`-marked gRPC responses memoized for one refresh cycle. | Fewer IPC round-trips |
| **WorkflowEngine on-demand + dedup** | Child process spawned only for hardening checks, one instance max. | No permanent child process |
| **`SelfContained=true` + shared `./publish/`** | .NET 10 runtime is bundled (no preinstall needed) but the three publishes overwrite identical runtime DLLs in the same folder. | Self-sufficient install + a single physical copy of the runtime on disk |

### Timing knobs

| Setting | Default | Location |
|---------|---------|----------|
| `RepetitionInterval` | `PT6H` | `Scheduler.json` |
| `RandomDelay` | `PT15M` | `Scheduler.json` |
| `DataServiceCacheTimeout` | `10 s` | `ComplianceApp/appsettings.json` |
| `CacheTtls.Default` | `1 min` | `ComplianceService` (code) |
| `RefreshTimeout` | — | `ComplianceApp/appsettings.json` |
| `WorkflowEngineStartupTimeout` | — | `ComplianceService/appsettings.json` |
| `OperationTimeout` | — | `ComplianceService/appsettings.json` |

## Technology Stack

| Concern | Technology | Used by |
|---------|-----------|---------|
| Runtime | .NET 10 / `net10.0-windows` | All main projects |
| UI framework | WPF | Client |
| IPC transport | gRPC over HTTP/2 (Kestrel) | Client + Service |
| Proto tooling | Grpc.Tools / Google.Protobuf | ComplianceApp.Protos |
| Logging | Serilog (file + console) | Client + Service |
| JSON serialization | Newtonsoft.Json | Client + Service |
| Scripting | MoonSharp (Lua 5.2) | Client — evaluation engine |
| HTTP resilience | Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Resilience (Polly) | Client + Service |
| Process execution | CliWrap | Service — WorkflowEngine launcher |
| CLI parsing | System.CommandLine | Client |
| DI container | Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection | Client + Service |
| Installer | Inno Setup 6 (EXE) | Setup |
| Test framework | MSTest + Moq | ComplianceApp.Tests |
| WMI/CIM access | SDH.Components.Windows | Service |

## Configuration & Deployment

### Settings files

| File | Bound to | Key settings |
|------|----------|-------------|
| `ComplianceService/appsettings.json` | `ServiceSettings` | gRPC endpoint, WorkflowEngine URL, SDH API credentials |
| `ComplianceApp/appsettings.json` | `AppSettings` | gRPC address, export targets, timeouts |
| `ComplianceApp/Themes/Resources/Colors.xaml` | Static WPF `ResourceDictionary` | Brand palette + status palette + semantic brushes (UI theme) |
| `Resources/Definitions/Win10.json` | Definition model | Tabs, categories, items, transformer keys, test conditions, Lua predicates |
| `Resources/Definitions/Scheduler.json` | `ScheduledTasksConfig` | Task names, triggers, allowed executables |

### Installer (Inno Setup 6)

> **Pivot release 2.6.0** — switched from WiX MSI to Inno Setup EXE due to
> the FireGiant OSMFEULA licence change. See
> [`Setup/README.md#why-inno-setup`](Setup/README.md#why-inno-setup).

```
publish-all-innosetup.ps1
  ├── dotnet publish ComplianceService   ─┐
  ├── dotnet publish ComplianceApp        ├─► ./publish/   ◄── Inno harvest
  └── dotnet publish WorkflowEngine.App  ─┘
                                              │
                                    Setup/ComplianceSetup.iss
                                              │
                                    ComplianceSetup-<Version>.exe (x64)
```

- `ComplianceService.exe` → registered as a Windows service (LocalSystem,
  auto-start, restart policy 3 x 60 s) by `ServiceManagement.iss`.
- All other binaries harvested by `[Files] Source: "publish\*"; Recursive`.
- `Resources\Definitions\*.json` are filtered per perimeter (Office / RnD /
  MnS) via dedicated `[Files]` entries with `Check: PerimeterIs(...)`.
- A bridge in `Scripts/LegacyMsiUninstall.iss` silently `msiexec /x`'s any
  pre-existing WiX MSI deployment (3 historical UpgradeCodes scanned via
  `MsiEnumRelatedProducts`) before the Inno install proceeds.
- Single source of truth for the version: `/Directory.Build.props`
  `<Version>`, read by `publish-all-innosetup.ps1` and injected into ISCC
  via `/DMyAppVersion=`.
- Platform: **x64** only.

### Logging

| Component | Log file |
|-----------|----------|
| ComplianceApp (client) | `C:\SMSLOGS\ComplianceApp.log` |
| ComplianceService (backend) | `C:\SMSLOGS\ComplianceService.log` |

---

## Key Invariants

> [!WARNING]
> **Never change** the Inno `MyAppId` `{CA9A7A52-9076-42BB-95F0-FD2B3A374210}` in
> `Setup/ComplianceSetup.iss`. Changing it breaks Inno's upgrade detection for every
> existing customer (new installs would coexist with old ones in ARP).
>
> Likewise, do not modify the three legacy `UpgradeCode` GUIDs hardcoded in
> `Setup/Scripts/LegacyMsiUninstall.iss` — they are the only way the Inno installer
> can detect and silently remove pre-2.6.0 MSI deployments still on customer machines.

> [!NOTE]
> **Shared publish folder:** All three executables publish to `./publish/` with
> `SelfContained=true`. Each publish bundles the .NET 10 runtime, but because
> the three publishes target the same folder they overwrite identical runtime
> DLLs — the final layout therefore contains a single physical copy of the
> runtime, while remaining self-sufficient (no preinstalled .NET required on
> the target machine).

> [!NOTE]
> **Version alignment:** the product version is centralised in
> `Directory.Build.props` (`<Version>` element, currently `2.6.0`). It flows
> automatically into the .NET assemblies (via MSBuild inheritance) and into the
> Inno EXE through `publish-all-innosetup.ps1`, which reads the value and injects
> it into ISCC via `/DMyAppVersion=...`. Bump the version in **one** file only.

using ComplianceApp.Shared.Attributes;
using Newtonsoft.Json;

namespace ComplianceApp.Shared.DTOs.Firewall;

/// <summary>
/// UI- and JSON-facing projection for the <c>WfpFirewallView</c> compliance
/// grid. Each row is a deduplicated WFP filter ARB entry; its nested
/// <see cref="VariantDetails"/> collection backs the expandable row-details
/// pane.
///
/// <para>
/// 11 columns in fixed order (<c>OrderId</c>, <c>Direction</c>,
/// <c>Name</c>, <c>ProviderName</c>, <c>LayerNameNormalized</c>,
/// <c>SubLayerName</c>, <c>Action</c>, <c>HasClearActionRight</c>,
/// <c>Conditions</c>, <c>ConditionsJson</c>, <c>VariantDetails</c>).
/// Hidden columns: <c>HasClearActionRight</c>, <c>ConditionsJson</c>,
/// <c>VariantDetails</c>.
/// <see cref="Conditions"/> is the stretch
/// (<see cref="FillColumnAttribute"/>) column.
/// </para>
///
/// <para>
/// <see cref="VariantDetails"/> is marked <see cref="HiddenColumnAttribute"/>
/// so <see cref="ComplianceApp.Wpf.Controls.SmartDataGrid"/> skips the auto-generated
/// column, but the property is still bindable from the row-details
/// <c>DataTemplate</c> (<c>{Binding VariantDetails}</c>). The nested list is
/// kept in the JSON payload so snapshot consumers can drill down naturally.
/// </para>
///
/// <para>
/// The property is deliberately typed as a <c>T[]</c> (not
/// <see cref="System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{T}"/>) so Newtonsoft's
/// <c>TypeNameHandling.Auto</c> root setting does not inject a
/// <c>{ "$type": "<>z__ReadOnlyList`1[...]", "$values": [...] }</c> envelope
/// around the nested array. With an array declaration the runtime type equals
/// the declared type, so Auto stays silent and the JSON payload stays flat.
/// </para>
/// </summary>
public sealed record WfpFirewallRow
{
    [JsonProperty(Order = 1, DefaultValueHandling = DefaultValueHandling.Include)]
    public int OrderId { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 2)]
    public string? Direction { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 3)]
    public string? Name { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 4)]
    public string? ProviderName { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 5)]
    public string? LayerNameNormalized { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 6)]
    public string? SubLayerName { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 7)]
    public string? Action { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 8, DefaultValueHandling = DefaultValueHandling.Include)]
    [HiddenColumn]
    public bool HasClearActionRight { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 9)]
    [FillColumn]
    public string? Conditions { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 10)]
    [HiddenColumn]
    public string? ConditionsJson { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 11)]
    [HiddenColumn]
    public WfpFirewallVariantRow[] VariantDetails { get; init; } = [];
}

using ComplianceApp.Shared.Attributes;
using Newtonsoft.Json;

namespace ComplianceApp.Shared.DTOs.Firewall;

/// <summary>
/// Nested UI row surfaced as the row-details payload of a
/// <see cref="WfpFirewallRow"/>. One instance per WFP filter variant that
/// collapsed into the parent deduplicated entry.
///
/// <para>
/// 4 columns in fixed order (<c>FilterId</c>, <c>LayerName</c>, <c>IsBoottime</c>,
/// <c>ProviderContextDataBufferHex</c>).
/// <see cref="ProviderContextDataBufferHex"/> is the stretch
/// (<see cref="FillColumnAttribute"/>) column.
/// </para>
/// </summary>
public sealed record WfpFirewallVariantRow
{
    [JsonProperty(Order = 1, DefaultValueHandling = DefaultValueHandling.Include)]
    public ulong FilterId { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 2)]
    public string? LayerName { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 3, DefaultValueHandling = DefaultValueHandling.Include)]
    public bool IsBoottime { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 4)]
    [FillColumn]
    public string? ProviderContextDataBufferHex { get; init; }
}

using System;
using ComplianceApp.Shared.Attributes;
using Newtonsoft.Json;

namespace ComplianceApp.Shared.DTOs.Firewall;

/// <summary>
/// UI- and JSON-facing projection of <see cref="ComplianceApp.Shared.DTOs.WfpEnrichedNetEventDto"/>
/// for the WFP Net Events compliance grid.
///
/// <para>
/// 12 columns in fixed order (<c>Timestamp</c>, <c>Direction</c>,
/// <c>EventType</c>, <c>ProtocolName</c>, <c>LocalAddress</c>,
/// <c>LocalPort</c>, <c>RemoteAddress</c>, <c>RemotePort</c>, <c>AppId</c>,
/// <c>FilterId</c>, <c>FilterName</c>, <c>SubLayerName</c>).
/// No <see cref="FillColumnAttribute"/> column: with 12 columns the natural
/// content already saturates the viewport, so any star-stretched column would
/// look unbalanced. All columns size to <c>Auto</c>.
/// Transformer sorts <c>Timestamp DESC</c>.
/// </para>
///
/// <para>
/// <see cref="Timestamp"/> normalizes to <see cref="DateTimeKind.Utc"/> in its
/// <c>init</c> accessor so the <c>Z</c> suffix is always emitted.
/// </para>
///
/// <para>
/// <see cref="LocalPort"/> / <see cref="RemotePort"/> force
/// <see cref="DefaultValueHandling.Include"/> so ephemeral port <c>0</c> is
/// always emitted (never stripped).
/// </para>
/// </summary>
public sealed record WfpNetEventRow
{
    private readonly DateTime _timestamp;

    [JsonProperty(Order = 1)]
    public DateTime Timestamp
    {
        get => _timestamp;
        init => _timestamp = DateTime.SpecifyKind(value, DateTimeKind.Utc);
    }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 2)]
    public string? Direction { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 3)]
    public string? EventType { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 4)]
    public string? ProtocolName { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 5)]
    public string? LocalAddress { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 6, DefaultValueHandling = DefaultValueHandling.Include)]
    public ushort LocalPort { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 7)]
    public string? RemoteAddress { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 8, DefaultValueHandling = DefaultValueHandling.Include)]
    public ushort RemotePort { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 9)]
    public string? AppId { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 10)]
    public string? FilterId { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 11)]
    public string? FilterName { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 12)]
    public string? SubLayerName { get; init; }
}

FILE: C:\Users\Vin\source\repos\sdh-complianceapp\ComplianceApp\ComplianceApp.Shared\DTOs\Firewall\WfpSubLayerFilterRow.cs

using ComplianceApp.Shared.Attributes;
using Newtonsoft.Json;

namespace ComplianceApp.Shared.DTOs.Firewall;

/// <summary>
/// Nested UI row surfaced as the row-details payload of a
/// <see cref="WfpSubLayerRow"/>. One instance per filter attached to the
/// sublayer.
///
/// <para>
/// 9 columns in fixed order (<c>FilterId</c>, <c>Name</c>,
/// <c>EffectiveWeight</c>, <c>ProviderName</c>, <c>LayerName</c>,
/// <c>Action</c>, <c>IsBoottime</c>, <c>HasClearActionRight</c>,
/// <c>Conditions</c>).
/// <see cref="Conditions"/> is the stretch
/// (<see cref="FillColumnAttribute"/>) column;
/// <see cref="HasClearActionRight"/> is hidden in the grid but kept in the
/// JSON payload.
/// </para>
/// </summary>
public sealed record WfpSubLayerFilterRow
{
    [JsonProperty(Order = 1, DefaultValueHandling = DefaultValueHandling.Include)]
    public ulong FilterId { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 2)]
    public string? Name { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 3)]
    public string? EffectiveWeight { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 4)]
    public string? ProviderName { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 5)]
    public string? LayerName { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 6)]
    public string? Action { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 7, DefaultValueHandling = DefaultValueHandling.Include)]
    public bool IsBoottime { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 8, DefaultValueHandling = DefaultValueHandling.Include)]
    [HiddenColumn]
    public bool HasClearActionRight { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 9)]
    [FillColumn]
    public string? Conditions { get; init; }
}

FILE: C:\Users\Vin\source\repos\sdh-complianceapp\ComplianceApp\ComplianceApp.Shared\DTOs\Firewall\WfpSubLayerRow.cs
================================================================================
using ComplianceApp.Shared.Attributes;
using Newtonsoft.Json;

namespace ComplianceApp.Shared.DTOs.Firewall;

/// <summary>
/// UI- and JSON-facing projection for the <c>WfpSubLayerDetails</c> compliance
/// grid. Each row is a WFP sublayer group; its nested
/// <see cref="FilterDetails"/> collection backs the expandable row-details
/// pane.
///
/// <para>
/// 5 columns in fixed order (<c>SubLayerName</c>, <c>Weight</c>,
/// <c>SubLayerKey</c>, <c>Filters</c>, <c>FilterDetails</c>).
/// <see cref="SubLayerName"/> is the stretch
/// (<see cref="FillColumnAttribute"/>) column;
/// <see cref="FilterDetails"/> is hidden in the grid but kept available
/// for binding by the row-details <c>DataTemplate</c>.
/// </para>
///
/// <para>
/// <see cref="FilterDetails"/> is deliberately typed as a <c>T[]</c> (not
/// <see cref="System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{T}"/>) so Newtonsoft's
/// <c>TypeNameHandling.Auto</c> root setting does not inject a
/// <c>{ "$type": "<>z__ReadOnlyList`1[...]", "$values": [...] }</c> envelope
/// around the nested array. With an array declaration the runtime type equals
/// the declared type, so Auto stays silent and the JSON payload stays flat.
/// </para>
/// </summary>
public sealed record WfpSubLayerRow
{
    [JsonProperty(Order = 1)]
    [FillColumn]
    public string? SubLayerName { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 2, DefaultValueHandling = DefaultValueHandling.Include)]
    public ushort Weight { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 3)]
    public string? SubLayerKey { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 4, DefaultValueHandling = DefaultValueHandling.Include)]
    public int Filters { get; init; }

    [JsonProperty(Order = 5)]
    [HiddenColumn]
    public WfpSubLayerFilterRow[] FilterDetails { get; init; } = [];
}


================================================================================
FILE: C:\Users\Vin\source\repos\sdh-complianceapp\ComplianceApp\ComplianceApp.Shared\DTOs\NetFwProduct.cs
================================================================================
using System.Runtime.Serialization;

namespace ComplianceApp.Shared.DTOs;

/// <summary>Managed projection for a firewall product row. Immutable to callers. Factory-only creation.</summary>
[DataContract]
public sealed class NetFwProduct
{
    /// <summary>Product display name.</summary>
    [DataMember(Order = 1, EmitDefaultValue = true)]
    public string Name { get; private set; } = default!;

    /// <summary>Path to the signed executable of the product.</summary>
    [DataMember(Order = 2, EmitDefaultValue = false)]
    public string PathToSignedProductExe { get; private set; } = default!;

    /// <summary>Firewall rule categories associated with the product.</summary>
    [DataMember(Order = 3, EmitDefaultValue = false)]
    public uint[] RuleCategories { get; private set; } = default!;

    [System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.SuppressMessage("Style", "IDE0046:Convert to conditional expression", Justification = "Readability")]
    public static NetFwProduct Create(
        string name,
        string pathToSignedProductExe,
        uint[] ruleCategories)
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(name))
            throw new ArgumentException("Value cannot be null or whitespace.", nameof(name));

        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(pathToSignedProductExe))
            throw new ArgumentException("Value cannot be null or whitespace.", nameof(pathToSignedProductExe));

        if (ruleCategories is null || ruleCategories.Length == 0)
            throw new ArgumentException("Value cannot be null or empty.", nameof(ruleCategories));

        return new NetFwProduct(
            name.Trim(),
            pathToSignedProductExe.Trim(),
            ruleCategories);
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Serialization constructor. Non-public to preserve factory usage.
    /// </summary>
    [System.Text.Json.Serialization.JsonConstructor] // non-public is honored
    [Newtonsoft.Json.JsonConstructor] // non-public is honored
    private NetFwProduct(
        string name,
        string pathToSignedProductExe,
        uint[] ruleCategories)
    {
        Name = name;
        PathToSignedProductExe = pathToSignedProductExe;
        RuleCategories = [.. ruleCategories];
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Optional non-public parameterless ctor for serializers that require it.
    /// </summary>
    private NetFwProduct()
    {
        // Used by serializers/ef that require a parameterless constructor.
    }
}

namespace ComplianceApp.Shared.DTOs;

public sealed record WfpEngineOptionDto(
    string Name,
    uint Value,
    string Description);

public sealed record WfpEnrichedFilterDto(
    string FilterKey,
    ulong FilterId,
    string Name,
    string FlagsValue,
    string Flags,
    ulong EffectiveWeightNumeric,
    string EffectiveWeight,
    int ConditionsCount,
    string ConditionsJson,
    string Action,
    bool IsBoottime,
    bool HasClearActionRight,
    string LayerKey,
    string LayerName,
    ushort LayerOrder,
    string SubLayerKey,
    string SubLayerName,
    ushort SubLayerWeight,
    string ProviderKey,
    string ProviderName,
    string ProviderDescription,
    string CalloutKey,
    string CalloutName,
    string ProviderContextKey,
    string ProviderContextName,
    ulong ProviderContextId,
    uint ProviderContextType,
    string ProviderContextTypeName,
    string ProviderContextDataHex,
    string ProviderContextDataBufferHex);

public sealed record WfpEnrichedNetEventDto(
    string Timestamp,
    string EventType,
    uint IpVersion,
    byte IpProtocol,
    string ProtocolName,
    string LocalAddress,
    ushort LocalPort,
    string RemoteAddress,
    ushort RemotePort,
    string AppId,
    string UserId,
    ulong? FilterId,
    ushort? LayerId,
    string LayerName,
    uint Flags,
    string FlagsDecoded,
    WfpEnrichedFilterDto? Filter);

public sealed record WfpEnrichedStateDto(
    IReadOnlyList<WfpEngineOptionDto> EngineOptions,
    IReadOnlyList<WfpEnrichedFilterDto> Filters,
    IReadOnlyList<WfpEnrichedNetEventDto> NetEvents);

FILE: C:\Users\Vin\source\repos\sdh-complianceapp\ComplianceApp\ComplianceApp.Shared\DTOs\WindowsDefenderFirewallPolicyInfo.cs

using System.Runtime.Serialization;

namespace ComplianceApp.Shared.DTOs;

/// <summary>Managed projection for Windows Defender Firewall policy information. Immutable to callers. Factory-only creation.</summary>
[DataContract]
public sealed class WindowsDefenderFirewallPolicyInfo
{
    [DataMember(Order = 1, EmitDefaultValue = true)]
    public string CurrentProfile { get; private set; } = default!;

    [DataMember(Order = 2, EmitDefaultValue = false)]
    public bool State { get; private set; }

    [DataMember(Order = 3, EmitDefaultValue = false)]
    public bool DomainState { get; private set; }

    [DataMember(Order = 4, EmitDefaultValue = false)]
    public bool PrivateState { get; private set; }

    [DataMember(Order = 5, EmitDefaultValue = false)]
    public bool PublicState { get; private set; }

    /// <summary>
    /// Factory. Creates from the provided values with rudimentary validation.
    /// </summary>
    public static WindowsDefenderFirewallPolicyInfo Create(
        string currentProfile,
        bool state = false,
        bool domainState = false,
        bool privateState = false,
        bool publicState = false)
    {
        // Basic null / whitespace validation
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(currentProfile))
            throw new ArgumentException("CurrentProfile cannot be null or whitespace.", nameof(currentProfile));

        // Normalize / trim
        currentProfile = currentProfile.Trim();

        return new WindowsDefenderFirewallPolicyInfo(
            currentProfile,
            state,
            domainState,
            privateState,
            publicState);
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Serialization constructor. Non-public to preserve factory usage.
    /// </summary>
    [System.Text.Json.Serialization.JsonConstructor] // non-public is honored
    [Newtonsoft.Json.JsonConstructor] // non-public is honored
    private WindowsDefenderFirewallPolicyInfo(
        string currentProfile,
        bool state,
        bool domainState,
        bool privateState,
        bool publicState)
    {
        CurrentProfile = currentProfile ?? string.Empty;
        State = state;
        DomainState = domainState;
        PrivateState = privateState;
        PublicState = publicState;
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Optional non-public parameterless ctor for serializers that require it.
    /// Not used by the factory.
    /// </summary>
    private WindowsDefenderFirewallPolicyInfo()
    {
        // Used by serializers that require a parameterless ctor.
    }
}


================================================================================
FILE: C:\Users\Vin\source\repos\sdh-complianceapp\ComplianceApp\ComplianceApp.Shared\Enums\NetFirewallRuleCategory.cs
================================================================================
using System.Runtime.Serialization;

namespace ComplianceApp.Shared.Enums;

/// <summary>
/// Represents the firewall rule categories.  
/// Values align with Win32 <c>NET_FW_RULE_CATEGORY_*</c> constants.
/// </summary>
[DataContract]
public enum NetFirewallRuleCategory : uint
{
    /// <summary>Specifies boot time filters. Win32: NET_FW_RULE_CATEGORY_BOOT (0).</summary>
    [EnumMember(Value = "NetFirewallRuleCategoryBoot")]
    NetFirewallRuleCategoryBoot = 0,

    /// <summary>Specifies stealth filters. Win32: NET_FW_RULE_CATEGORY_STEALTH (1).</summary>
    [EnumMember(Value = "NetFirewallRuleCategoryStealth")]
    NetFirewallRuleCategoryStealth = 1,

    /// <summary>Specifies firewall filters. Win32: NET_FW_RULE_CATEGORY_FIREWALL (2).</summary>
    [EnumMember(Value = "NetFirewallRuleCategoryFirewall")]
    NetFirewallRuleCategoryFirewall = 2,

    /// <summary>Specifies connection security filters. Win32: NET_FW_RULE_CATEGORY_CONSEC (3).</summary>
    [EnumMember(Value = "NetFirewallRuleCategoryConSec")]
    NetFirewallRuleCategoryConSec = 3,

    /// <summary>Maximum value for testing purposes. Win32: NET_FW_RULE_CATEGORY_MAX (4).</summary>
    [EnumMember(Value = "NetFirewallRuleCategoryMax")]
    NetFirewallRuleCategoryMax = 4,

    /// <summary>Unknown or unsupported category. Used as a guard for forward compatibility.</summary>
    [EnumMember(Value = "Unknown")]
    Unknown = 0xFFFFFFFF
}


================================================================================
FILE: C:\Users\Vin\source\repos\sdh-complianceapp\ComplianceApp\ComplianceApp.Tests\Services\Grpc\FirewallAdapterTests.cs
================================================================================
extern alias Proto;
using System;
using System.Threading;
using System.Threading.Tasks;
using ComplianceApp.Services.Grpc;
using ComplianceApp.Shared.Enums;
using Grpc.Core;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;
using Moq;
using ProtoSvc = Proto::Compliance.V1.ComplianceService;
using ProtoMessages = Proto::Compliance.V1;

namespace ComplianceApp.Tests.Services.Grpc;

/// <summary>
/// Tests for the <c>Firewall</c> partial of <see cref="GrpcDataServiceAdapter"/>.
///
/// <para>This partial mixes three independent projection patterns that all
/// deserve dedicated coverage:</para>
/// <list type="bullet">
/// <item><description><c>SecurityCenterProduct</c> projection — the proto
/// stores the three status uints as DECIMAL strings; the adapter calls
/// <see cref="uint.Parse(string)"/> which throws on non-digits. Verifying
/// the happy path freezes the contract.</description></item>
/// <item><description><c>NetFwProduct</c> projection — array materialization
/// preserving order, with <see cref="ProtoMessages.NetFwProduct.RuleCategories"/>
/// converted via collection expression.</description></item>
/// <item><description><c>WfpEnrichedStateDto</c> projection — the heaviest
/// composite of the whole adapter, including a nested <c>Filter</c> reference
/// inside each <c>NetEvent</c> that may legitimately be null.</description></item>
/// </list>
/// </summary>
[TestClass]
public class FirewallAdapterTests
{
    private Mock<ProtoSvc.ComplianceServiceClient> _client = null!;
    private GrpcDataServiceAdapter _sut = null!;

    [TestInitialize]
    public void Setup()
    {
        _client = new Mock<ProtoSvc.ComplianceServiceClient>();
        _sut    = new GrpcDataServiceAdapter(_client.Object, Mock.Of<ILogger<GrpcDataServiceAdapter>>());
    }

    // ─── SecurityCenter products (S1 / SEP) ──────────────────────────────────

    [TestMethod]
    public async Task GetSentinelOneSecurityCenterFirewallProduct_OuterNull_ReturnsNull()
    {
        _client.Setup(c => c.GetSentinelOneSecurityCenterFirewallProductAsync(
                It.IsAny<ProtoMessages.GetSentinelOneSecurityCenterFirewallProductRequest>(),
                It.IsAny<Metadata?>(), It.IsAny<DateTime?>(), It.IsAny<CancellationToken>()))
            .Returns(GrpcTestHelpers.AsCall(new ProtoMessages.GetSentinelOneSecurityCenterFirewallProductResponse()));

        Assert.IsNull(await _sut.GetSentinelOneSecurityCenterFirewallProduct());
    }

    [TestMethod]
    public async Task GetSentinelOneSecurityCenterFirewallProduct_Populated_ParsesUintStrings()
    {
        _client.Setup(c => c.GetSentinelOneSecurityCenterFirewallProductAsync(
                It.IsAny<ProtoMessages.GetSentinelOneSecurityCenterFirewallProductRequest>(),
                It.IsAny<Metadata?>(), It.IsAny<DateTime?>(), It.IsAny<CancellationToken>()))
            .Returns(GrpcTestHelpers.AsCall(new ProtoMessages.GetSentinelOneSecurityCenterFirewallProductResponse
            {
                Product = new ProtoMessages.SecurityCenterProduct
                {
                    Name                   = "SentinelOne",
                    PathToSignedProductExe = @"C:\S1\agent.exe",
                    ProductState           = 0x06_11_00u,
                    ProductStatus          = "0",
                    SignatureStatus        = "1",
                    ProductOwner           = "2",
                },
            }));

        var dto = await _sut.GetSentinelOneSecurityCenterFirewallProduct();

        Assert.IsNotNull(dto);
        Assert.AreEqual("SentinelOne", dto.Name);
        Assert.AreEqual(0x06_11_00u,    dto.ProductState);
        Assert.AreEqual(0u,             dto.ProductStatus);
        Assert.AreEqual(1u,             dto.SignatureStatus);
        Assert.AreEqual(2u,             dto.ProductOwner);
    }

    [TestMethod]
    public async Task GetSepSecurityCenterFirewallProduct_OuterNull_ReturnsNull()
    {
        _client.Setup(c => c.GetSepSecurityCenterFirewallProductAsync(
                It.IsAny<ProtoMessages.GetSepSecurityCenterFirewallProductRequest>(),
                It.IsAny<Metadata?>(), It.IsAny<DateTime?>(), It.IsAny<CancellationToken>()))
            .Returns(GrpcTestHelpers.AsCall(new ProtoMessages.GetSepSecurityCenterFirewallProductResponse()));

        Assert.IsNull(await _sut.GetSepSecurityCenterFirewallProduct());
    }

    // ─── NetFwProducts ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    [TestMethod]
    public async Task GetNetFwProducts_NonEmpty_ProjectsAndPreservesOrder()
    {
        var response = new ProtoMessages.GetNetFwProductsResponse();
        var p1 = new ProtoMessages.NetFwProduct
        {
            Name = "Defender", PathToSignedProductExe = @"C:\Windows\System32\msmpeng.exe",
        };
        p1.RuleCategories.AddRange(new uint[] { 0u, 1u, 2u, 3u });
        var p2 = new ProtoMessages.NetFwProduct
        {
            Name = "ThirdParty FW", PathToSignedProductExe = @"C:\TP\fw.exe",
        };
        p2.RuleCategories.Add(0u);
        response.Products.Add(p1);
        response.Products.Add(p2);
        _client.Setup(c => c.GetNetFwProductsAsync(
                It.IsAny<ProtoMessages.GetNetFwProductsRequest>(),
                It.IsAny<Metadata?>(), It.IsAny<DateTime?>(), It.IsAny<CancellationToken>()))
            .Returns(GrpcTestHelpers.AsCall(response));

        var products = await _sut.GetNetFwProducts();

        Assert.HasCount(2, products);
        Assert.AreEqual("Defender",     products[0].Name);
        Assert.AreEqual("ThirdParty FW", products[1].Name);
        CollectionAssert.AreEqual(new uint[] { 0, 1, 2, 3 }, products[0].RuleCategories);
        CollectionAssert.AreEqual(new uint[] { 0 },          products[1].RuleCategories);
    }

    [TestMethod]
    public async Task GetNetFwProducts_Empty_ReturnsEmptyArray()
    {
        _client.Setup(c => c.GetNetFwProductsAsync(
                It.IsAny<ProtoMessages.GetNetFwProductsRequest>(),
                It.IsAny<Metadata?>(), It.IsAny<DateTime?>(), It.IsAny<CancellationToken>()))
            .Returns(GrpcTestHelpers.AsCall(new ProtoMessages.GetNetFwProductsResponse()));

        var products = await _sut.GetNetFwProducts();

        Assert.IsNotNull(products);
        Assert.IsEmpty(products,
            "Adapter pre-allocates a fixed-length array sized from the proto repeated count — empty repeated → length-0 array, never null.");
    }

    // ─── Net connection profile name ─────────────────────────────────────────

    [TestMethod]
    public async Task GetNetConnectionProfileName_HasName_ReturnsThatString()
    {
        _client.Setup(c => c.GetNetConnectionProfileNameAsync(
                It.IsAny<ProtoMessages.GetNetConnectionProfileNameRequest>(),
                It.IsAny<Metadata?>(), It.IsAny<DateTime?>(), It.IsAny<CancellationToken>()))
            .Returns(GrpcTestHelpers.AsCall(new ProtoMessages.GetNetConnectionProfileNameResponse { Name = "Domain" }));

        Assert.AreEqual("Domain", await _sut.GetNetConnectionProfileName());
    }

    [TestMethod]
    public async Task GetNetConnectionProfileName_NotSet_ReturnsNull()
    {
        _client.Setup(c => c.GetNetConnectionProfileNameAsync(
                It.IsAny<ProtoMessages.GetNetConnectionProfileNameRequest>(),
                It.IsAny<Metadata?>(), It.IsAny<DateTime?>(), It.IsAny<CancellationToken>()))
            .Returns(GrpcTestHelpers.AsCall(new ProtoMessages.GetNetConnectionProfileNameResponse()));

        Assert.IsNull(await _sut.GetNetConnectionProfileName());
    }

    // ─── NetFirewallProfileState ─────────────────────────────────────────────

    [TestMethod]
    public async Task GetNetFirewallProfileState_ForwardsProfileName()
    {
        ProtoMessages.GetNetFirewallProfileStateRequest? captured = null;
        _client.Setup(c => c.GetNetFirewallProfileStateAsync(
                It.IsAny<ProtoMessages.GetNetFirewallProfileStateRequest>(),
                It.IsAny<Metadata?>(), It.IsAny<DateTime?>(), It.IsAny<CancellationToken>()))
            .Callback<ProtoMessages.GetNetFirewallProfileStateRequest, Metadata, DateTime?, CancellationToken>(
                (r, _, _, _) => captured = r)
            .Returns(GrpcTestHelpers.AsCall(new ProtoMessages.GetNetFirewallProfileStateResponse
            {
                State = ProtoMessages.FirewallState.On,
            }));

        await _sut.GetNetFirewallProfileState("Domain");

        Assert.AreEqual("Domain", captured!.ProfileName);
    }

    [TestMethod]
    [DataRow(ProtoMessages.FirewallState.On,  FirewallState.On)]
    [DataRow(ProtoMessages.FirewallState.Off, FirewallState.Off)]
    public async Task GetNetFirewallProfileState_HasState_ProjectsEnum(
        ProtoMessages.FirewallState protoState, FirewallState expected)
    {
        _client.Setup(c => c.GetNetFirewallProfileStateAsync(
                It.IsAny<ProtoMessages.GetNetFirewallProfileStateRequest>(),
                It.IsAny<Metadata?>(), It.IsAny<DateTime?>(), It.IsAny<CancellationToken>()))
            .Returns(GrpcTestHelpers.AsCall(new ProtoMessages.GetNetFirewallProfileStateResponse { State = protoState }));

        Assert.AreEqual(expected, await _sut.GetNetFirewallProfileState("Any"));
    }

    [TestMethod]
    public async Task GetNetFirewallProfileState_UnknownProto255_DoesNotMatchSharedUnknown()
    {
        // KNOWN MISMATCH (worth pinning for visibility): the shared
        // FirewallState enum uses sequential values (Off=0, On=1, Unknown=2)
        // while the proto contract uses 255 for Unknown; the adapter cast
        // (SharedEnums.FirewallState)(int)r.State surfaces 255 verbatim, NOT
        // FirewallState.Unknown. Either the proto enum needs to be aligned
        // (Unknown=2) or the adapter needs a switch — until either lands,
        // this test documents the current behavior so a future fix is
        // intentional, not accidental.
        _client.Setup(c => c.GetNetFirewallProfileStateAsync(
                It.IsAny<ProtoMessages.GetNetFirewallProfileStateRequest>(),
                It.IsAny<Metadata?>(), It.IsAny<DateTime?>(), It.IsAny<CancellationToken>()))
            .Returns(GrpcTestHelpers.AsCall(new ProtoMessages.GetNetFirewallProfileStateResponse
            {
                State = ProtoMessages.FirewallState.Unknown,
            }));

        var actual = await _sut.GetNetFirewallProfileState("Any");

        Assert.AreEqual((FirewallState)255, actual);
        Assert.AreNotEqual(FirewallState.Unknown, actual);
    }

    [TestMethod]
    public async Task GetNetFirewallProfileState_NoState_ReturnsNull()
    {
        _client.Setup(c => c.GetNetFirewallProfileStateAsync(
                It.IsAny<ProtoMessages.GetNetFirewallProfileStateRequest>(),
                It.IsAny<Metadata?>(), It.IsAny<DateTime?>(), It.IsAny<CancellationToken>()))
            .Returns(GrpcTestHelpers.AsCall(new ProtoMessages.GetNetFirewallProfileStateResponse()));

        Assert.IsNull(await _sut.GetNetFirewallProfileState("Any"));
    }

    // ─── WfpEnrichedState (heaviest composite) ───────────────────────────────

    [TestMethod]
    public async Task GetWfpEnrichedState_EmptyResponse_ReturnsEmptyCollections()
    {
        _client.Setup(c => c.GetWfpEnrichedStateAsync(
                It.IsAny<ProtoMessages.GetWfpEnrichedStateRequest>(),
                It.IsAny<Metadata?>(), It.IsAny<DateTime?>(), It.IsAny<CancellationToken>()))
            .Returns(GrpcTestHelpers.AsCall(new ProtoMessages.GetWfpEnrichedStateResponse()));

        var dto = await _sut.GetWfpEnrichedState();

        Assert.IsEmpty(dto.EngineOptions);
        Assert.IsEmpty(dto.Filters);
        Assert.IsEmpty(dto.NetEvents);
    }

    [TestMethod]
    public async Task GetWfpEnrichedState_PopulatedFilter_ProjectsCoreFields()
    {
        var response = new ProtoMessages.GetWfpEnrichedStateResponse();
        response.EngineOptions.Add(new ProtoMessages.WfpEngineOption
        {
            Name = "FWPM_ENGINE_NET_EVENT_MATCH_ANY_KEYWORDS", Value = 0u, Description = "default",
        });
        var filter = new ProtoMessages.WfpEnrichedFilter
        {
            FilterKey               = "{abcd}",
            FilterId                = 12345UL,
            Name                    = "Block port 445",
            FlagsValue              = "0x0",
            Flags                   = "",
            EffectiveWeightNumeric  = 0xFFFF_0000_0000_0000UL,
            EffectiveWeight         = "Highest",
            ConditionsCount         = 2,
            ConditionsJson          = "[{...}]",
            Action                  = "Block",
            IsBoottime              = false,
            HasClearActionRight     = true,
            LayerKey                = "{layer-key}",
            LayerName               = "ALE_AUTH_RECV_ACCEPT_V4",
            LayerOrder              = 7u,
            SubLayerKey             = "{sublayer-key}",
            SubLayerName            = "WindowsFirewall",
            SubLayerWeight          = 32_767u,
            ProviderKey             = "{provider-key}",
            ProviderName            = "MpsSvc",
            ProviderDescription     = "Microsoft Defender Firewall",
            CalloutKey              = "",
            CalloutName             = "",
            ProviderContextKey      = "{ctx-key}",
            ProviderContextName     = "ctx-name",
            ProviderContextId       = 99UL,
            ProviderContextType     = 1u,
            ProviderContextTypeName = "FWPM_PROVIDER_CONTEXT_TYPE_KEYING_MODULE",
            ProviderContextDataHex  = "00 11 22",
            ProviderContextDataBufferHex = "AA BB",
        };
        response.Filters.Add(filter);

        response.NetEvents.Add(new ProtoMessages.WfpEnrichedNetEvent
        {
            Timestamp     = "2026-04-30T18:30:00Z",
            EventType     = "FWPM_NET_EVENT_TYPE_CLASSIFY_DROP",
            IpVersion     = 4u,
            IpProtocol    = 6u,
            ProtocolName  = "TCP",
            LocalAddress  = "10.0.0.1",
            LocalPort     = 49_152u,
            RemoteAddress = "1.1.1.1",
            RemotePort    = 443u,
            AppId         = "system32\\svchost.exe",
            UserId        = "S-1-5-19",
            FilterId      = 12345UL,
            LayerId       = 100u,
            LayerName     = "ALE_AUTH_RECV_ACCEPT_V4",
            Flags         = 0u,
            FlagsDecoded  = "",
            Filter        = filter,  // nested filter ref → projected
        });

        _client.Setup(c => c.GetWfpEnrichedStateAsync(
                It.IsAny<ProtoMessages.GetWfpEnrichedStateRequest>(),
                It.IsAny<Metadata?>(), It.IsAny<DateTime?>(), It.IsAny<CancellationToken>()))
            .Returns(GrpcTestHelpers.AsCall(response));

        var dto = await _sut.GetWfpEnrichedState();

        Assert.HasCount(1, dto.EngineOptions);
        Assert.AreEqual("FWPM_ENGINE_NET_EVENT_MATCH_ANY_KEYWORDS", dto.EngineOptions[0].Name);

        Assert.HasCount(1,                       dto.Filters);
        var f = dto.Filters[0];
        Assert.AreEqual(12345UL,                 f.FilterId);
        Assert.AreEqual("Block port 445",        f.Name);
        Assert.AreEqual("Block",                 f.Action);
        Assert.AreEqual((ushort)7,               f.LayerOrder,
            "Proto carries LayerOrder as uint32 but the DTO is ushort — adapter casts down; covers values <= 65535.");
        Assert.AreEqual((ushort)32_767,          f.SubLayerWeight);

        Assert.HasCount(1,                       dto.NetEvents);
        var e = dto.NetEvents[0];
        Assert.AreEqual("2026-04-30T18:30:00Z",  e.Timestamp);
        Assert.AreEqual((byte)6,                 e.IpProtocol,
            "IpProtocol cast from uint32 → byte; the proto can carry larger values but TCP=6 fits.");
        Assert.AreEqual((ushort)443,             e.RemotePort);
        Assert.AreEqual(12345UL,                 e.FilterId);
        Assert.AreEqual((ushort)100,             e.LayerId);
        Assert.IsNotNull(e.Filter);
        Assert.AreEqual("Block port 445",        e.Filter!.Name);
    }

    [TestMethod]
    public async Task GetWfpEnrichedState_NetEventWithoutFilterReference_ProjectsFilterAsNull()
    {
        var response = new ProtoMessages.GetWfpEnrichedStateResponse();
        response.NetEvents.Add(new ProtoMessages.WfpEnrichedNetEvent
        {
            Timestamp = "2026-04-30T18:30:00Z",
            EventType = "FWPM_NET_EVENT_TYPE_CLASSIFY_DROP",
            // FilterId / LayerId left unset → optional message null
        });
        _client.Setup(c => c.GetWfpEnrichedStateAsync(
                It.IsAny<ProtoMessages.GetWfpEnrichedStateRequest>(),
                It.IsAny<Metadata?>(), It.IsAny<DateTime?>(), It.IsAny<CancellationToken>()))
            .Returns(GrpcTestHelpers.AsCall(response));

        var dto = await _sut.GetWfpEnrichedState();

        Assert.HasCount(1, dto.NetEvents);
        var e = dto.NetEvents[0];
        Assert.IsNull(e.Filter,
            "Optional Filter message left unset on the wire → null on the client; must NOT throw on the projection's null-conditional MapDtoFilter call.");
        Assert.IsNull(e.FilterId);
        Assert.IsNull(e.LayerId);
    }
}

using System;
using System.Linq;
using System.Threading;
using System.Threading.Tasks;
using ComplianceApp.Common.Extensions;
using ComplianceApp.Interfaces;
using ComplianceApp.Models;
using ComplianceApp.Shared.Contracts;
using ComplianceApp.Shared.Enums;

namespace ComplianceApp.DataTransformers;

public class FirewallRuleCategoryBootTime(IDataService dataService, IValueTester valueTester) : DataTransformer, IDataTransformer
{
    private readonly IDataService _dataService = dataService;
    private readonly IValueTester _valueTester = valueTester;

    public override async Task TransformAsync(Item item, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)
    {
        await item.TrySetValue(async () =>
        {
            var filteredProductNames = (await _dataService.GetNetFwProducts())
                .Where(product => product.RuleCategories.Contains((uint)NetFirewallRuleCategory.NetFirewallRuleCategoryBoot))
                .Select(product => product.Name);

            return !filteredProductNames.Any()
                 ? "Windows Defender Firewall"
                 : string.Join(Environment.NewLine, filteredProductNames);
        });

        item.SetCompliance(await _valueTester.EvaluateAsync(item.Value, item.TestCondition, item.TestValue));
    }
}

================================================================================
FILE: C:\Users\Vin\source\repos\sdh-complianceapp\ComplianceApp\ComplianceApp\DataTransformers\Firewall\WfpFirewallView.cs
================================================================================
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using System.Threading;
using System.Threading.Tasks;
using ComplianceApp.Interfaces;
using ComplianceApp.Models;
using ComplianceApp.Services;
using ComplianceApp.Shared.Contracts;
using ComplianceApp.Shared.DTOs;
using ComplianceApp.Shared.DTOs.Firewall;
using ComplianceApp.Shared.Enums.V2;

namespace ComplianceApp.DataTransformers;

public class WfpFirewallView(IDataService dataService) : DataTransformer, IDataTransformer
{
    private readonly IDataService _dataService = dataService;

    public override async Task TransformAsync(Item item, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)
    {
        await item.TrySetContent(async () =>
        {
            var state = await _dataService.GetWfpEnrichedState().ConfigureAwait(false);
            var filters = WfpFilterPipeline.Deduplicate(state.Filters);

            IReadOnlyList<WfpFirewallRow> rows = filters
                .Select((f, i) => new WfpFirewallRow
                {
                    OrderId = i + 1,
                    Direction = f.Direction,
                    Name = f.Name,
                    ProviderName = f.ProviderName,
                    LayerNameNormalized = f.LayerNameNormalized,
                    SubLayerName = f.SubLayerName,
                    Action = f.Action,
                    HasClearActionRight = f.HasClearActionRight,
                    Conditions = f.Conditions,
                    ConditionsJson = f.ConditionsJson,
                    VariantDetails = BuildVariantDetails(f.Variants),
                })
                .ToArray();

            return rows;
        }).ConfigureAwait(false);

        item.IsExpanded = (item.Content as IReadOnlyList<WfpFirewallRow>)?.Count is > 0 && item.IsExpanded;
        item.SetCompliance(Compliance.Hidden);
    }

    // Returns a concrete T[] (not an IReadOnlyList<T>): keeps Newtonsoft's
    // Auto TypeNameHandling silent by making the runtime type match the
    // property's declared array type. See WfpFirewallRow.VariantDetails for
    // the rationale.
    private static WfpFirewallVariantRow[] BuildVariantDetails(IReadOnlyList<WfpEnrichedFilterDto> variants) => [.. variants
        .Select(v => new WfpFirewallVariantRow
        {
            FilterId = v.FilterId,
            LayerName = v.LayerName,
            IsBoottime = v.IsBoottime,
            ProviderContextDataBufferHex = v.ProviderContextDataBufferHex,
        })];
}


================================================================================
FILE: C:\Users\Vin\source\repos\sdh-complianceapp\ComplianceApp\ComplianceApp\DataTransformers\Firewall\WfpNetEvents.cs
================================================================================
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Globalization;
using System.Linq;
using System.Threading;
using System.Threading.Tasks;
using ComplianceApp.Interfaces;
using ComplianceApp.Models;
using ComplianceApp.Services;
using ComplianceApp.Shared.Contracts;
using ComplianceApp.Shared.DTOs.Firewall;
using ComplianceApp.Shared.Enums.V2;

namespace ComplianceApp.DataTransformers;

public class WfpNetEvents(IDataService dataService) : DataTransformer, IDataTransformer
{
    private readonly IDataService _dataService = dataService;

    public override async Task TransformAsync(Item item, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)
    {
        await item.TrySetContent(async () =>
        {
            var state = await _dataService.GetWfpEnrichedState().ConfigureAwait(false);

            // Timestamp DESC — newest events first.
            IReadOnlyList<WfpNetEventRow> rows = state.NetEvents
                .OrderByDescending(e => e.Timestamp, StringComparer.Ordinal)
                .Select(e => new WfpNetEventRow
                {
                    Timestamp = DateTime.Parse(e.Timestamp, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, DateTimeStyles.RoundtripKind),
                    Direction = WfpFilterPipeline.GetLayerDirection(e.LayerName),
                    EventType = e.EventType,
                    ProtocolName = e.ProtocolName,
                    LocalAddress = e.LocalAddress,
                    LocalPort = e.LocalPort,
                    RemoteAddress = e.RemoteAddress,
                    RemotePort = e.RemotePort,
                    AppId = e.AppId,
                    FilterId = e.FilterId?.ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture),
                    FilterName = e.Filter?.Name,
                    SubLayerName = e.Filter?.SubLayerName,
                })
                .ToArray();

            return rows;
        }).ConfigureAwait(false);

        item.IsExpanded = (item.Content as IReadOnlyList<WfpNetEventRow>)?.Count is > 0 && item.IsExpanded;
        item.SetCompliance(Compliance.Hidden);
    }
}


================================================================================
FILE: C:\Users\Vin\source\repos\sdh-complianceapp\ComplianceApp\ComplianceApp\DataTransformers\Firewall\WfpSubLayerDetails.cs
================================================================================
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using System.Threading;
using System.Threading.Tasks;
using ComplianceApp.Interfaces;
using ComplianceApp.Models;
using ComplianceApp.Services;
using ComplianceApp.Shared.Contracts;
using ComplianceApp.Shared.DTOs;
using ComplianceApp.Shared.DTOs.Firewall;
using ComplianceApp.Shared.Enums.V2;

namespace ComplianceApp.DataTransformers;

public class WfpSubLayerDetails(IDataService dataService) : DataTransformer, IDataTransformer
{
    private readonly IDataService _dataService = dataService;

    public override async Task TransformAsync(Item item, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)
    {
        await item.TrySetContent(async () =>
        {
            var state = await _dataService.GetWfpEnrichedState().ConfigureAwait(false);

            // Group the enriched filter list by sublayer identity. Inside each
            // group filters are ordered LayerName ASC then effective weight
            // DESC (row-details grid rendering). Groups themselves are emitted
            // by SubLayer weight DESC.
            IReadOnlyList<WfpSubLayerRow> rows = [.. state.Filters
                .GroupBy(f => (f.SubLayerKey, f.SubLayerName, f.SubLayerWeight))
                .Select(g =>
                {
                    var ordered = g.OrderBy(f => f.LayerName)
                                   .ThenByDescending(f => f.EffectiveWeightNumeric)
                                   .ToArray();
                    return new WfpSubLayerRow
                    {
                        SubLayerName = g.Key.SubLayerName,
                        Weight = g.Key.SubLayerWeight,
                        SubLayerKey = g.Key.SubLayerKey,
                        Filters = ordered.Length,
                        FilterDetails = BuildFilterDetails(ordered),
                    };
                })
                .OrderByDescending(x => x.Weight)];

            return rows;
        }).ConfigureAwait(false);

        item.IsExpanded = (item.Content as IReadOnlyList<WfpSubLayerRow>)?.Count is > 0 && item.IsExpanded;
        item.SetCompliance(Compliance.Hidden);
    }

    // Returns a concrete T[] (not an IReadOnlyList<T>): keeps Newtonsoft's
    // Auto TypeNameHandling silent by making the runtime type match the
    // property's declared array type. See WfpSubLayerRow.FilterDetails for
    // the rationale.
    private static WfpSubLayerFilterRow[] BuildFilterDetails(IReadOnlyList<WfpEnrichedFilterDto> filters) => [.. filters
        .Select(f => new WfpSubLayerFilterRow
        {
            FilterId = f.FilterId,
            Name = f.Name,
            EffectiveWeight = f.EffectiveWeight,
            ProviderName = f.ProviderName,
            LayerName = f.LayerName,
            Action = f.Action,
            IsBoottime = f.IsBoottime,
            HasClearActionRight = f.HasClearActionRight,
            Conditions = WfpFilterPipeline.FormatFilterLogicCompact(f),
        })];
}
